title: Post hoc ergo propter hoc
wikitext:
[]: Post hoc ergo propter hoc             Quote Post by Felipe » Mon Feb 18, 2013 4:04 pm As discussed likewise in a recent topic, my attention was called to the idea that much of the way we think about Evangelion was made after the fact, thus the many inconsistencies and plainly obscure things we come upon when we try to think about the series (just what kind of life did Kaworu live? How was Yui and Gendo's relationship? How does one obtain and use a soul?, etc.), and it kept me wondering just how much of the show that we love wasn`t really there in the first place. An example that I like is the fact that you can tell quite clearly that Unit 01 was always supposed to be Yui in some way, but the incident where she was absorbed, the questions concerning the way in which this occurred, where thought much later, if so, even. Also, I have the very strong feeling that Instrumentality, although mentioned early on, wasn't really created until it came to pass. So, what do you guys think might fit into this category? What do you think Anno created after the fact (probably when he wrote the Classified Information for E2?)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Feb 18, 2013 4:38 pm It was sorta mentioned somewhere that Anno had not quite decided what exactly the instrumentality project would be, but the general idea was already there - it may originally looked a lot more like mass suicide, before there was an actual cult mass suicide and they changed things so it wouldn't lose its general applicability/ be branded as being about that mass suicide event. As with the Rebuilds, some things were probably made up as they went along, but others were planned- They hadn'd decided on Mari's personality until shortly before the last few drafts, but Ishida says in the Q booklet that all things Kaworu were planned from the very beginning, and that there are great plans. It's probably similar with the classic series. Also, there's the possibility of Anno putting in later things, but not still building upon things he built in previously, as the whole Rebuild Shinji-and-Rei-Being-Brought together thing. He has a hilariously weird relationship with writing Rei. On some level, he seems detached from her, but he claims that the "Poka poka" was Rei's own idea XD

[]:             Quote Post by Felipe » Mon Feb 18, 2013 4:53 pm Kendrix wrote: It was sorta mentioned somewhere that Anno had not quite decided what exactly the instrumentality project would be, but the general idea was already there - it may originally looked a lot more like mass suicide, before there was an actual cult mass suicide and they changed things so it wouldn't lose its general applicability/ be branded as being about that mass suicide event.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Feb 18, 2013 10:11 pm I don't think "some stuff was planned and other stuff wasn't" is much of a spoiler...

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Mon Feb 18, 2013 10:51 pm Reading the proposal, I got the impression that Instrumentality was going to be a SUPER-EVA UPGRADE! or a more cheery version of what it became. I think that Lilith wasn't conceived of until very late in the production. This would explain why Kaji calls her Adam. Note that Lilith is never named, not even in flashing words in the opening, until episode 24 (if I'm not mistaken).

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Feb 19, 2013 12:11 am There was always a lilith somewhere though... starting the numbering of Sachiel with 3rd and saying Adam is 1st necessitates they know where 2nd angel is (or was). Same thing with Asuka being implied right from the beginning via Shinji labeled 3rd child and Rei the 1st. It would be interesting to know how much of the 'tumbling down' arc starting around 14 was planned from the beginning vs. improvised as Anno's mood shifted mid-production.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Tue Feb 19, 2013 12:15 am NemZ wrote: There was always a lilith somewhere though... starting the numbering of Sachiel with 3rd and saying Adam is 1st necessitates they know where 2nd angel is (or was). Same thing with Asuka being implied right from the beginning via Shinji labeled 3rd child and Rei the 1st.

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Tue Feb 19, 2013 12:32 am Felipe wrote: Is that the Aum Shirinkyo thing?

[]:             Quote Post by Felipe » Tue Feb 19, 2013 5:34 am Kendrix wrote: I don't think "some stuff was planned and other stuff wasn't" is much of a spoiler...

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Feb 19, 2013 8:00 am Lavinius wrote: Not neccesarily, the Second Angel could have been intended to be the Lilim equivalent.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Mar 01, 2013 10:06 am Kendrix wrote: He has a hilariously weird relationship with writing Rei. On some level, he seems detached from her, but he claims that the "Poka poka" was Rei's own idea XD

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Mar 01, 2013 10:31 am kuribo-04 wrote: What is that "Poka Poka" thing?

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Mar 01, 2013 10:33 am riffraff11235 wrote: It's in the elevator scene in Rebuild, when Rei says that she wants Shinji to feel "warm and content", among other things.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Mar 01, 2013 10:38 am kuribo-04 wrote: Ah, thanks. But why "Poka Poka"? Is it an onomatopeia?

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Mar 01, 2013 10:41 am riffraff11235 wrote: In a way. Japanese uses a lot of these kinds of onomotopeic expressions. "Doki-doki" for heartbeat is a notable example. I think "poka-poka" is supposed to evoke the kind of soothing warmth you get from a fireplace.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Mar 01, 2013 11:00 am kuribo-04 wrote: There exists an onomatopeia for that?! They really have words and onomatopeias for everything.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Mar 01, 2013 11:10 am riffraff11235 wrote: Yeah, it's one of the language's many interesting features. This article might help to clear up the definition. Also, a word of advice: You may want to rearrange/shrink the text in your sig. It takes quite a bit of space like that.

[]: Re: Post hoc ergo propter hoc             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Mar 09, 2013 11:43 pm Felipe wrote: Also, I have the very strong feeling that Instrumentality, although mentioned early on, wasn't really created until it came to pass.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Mar 10, 2013 2:55 am IIRC, the information we have suggests that while the Instrumentaity maguffin may have been there from the beginning, exactly what is was didn't get decided until very late in production.

[]:             Quote Post by Maru » Wed Mar 13, 2013 10:59 pm Monk Ed wrote: Exactly. In fact, prior to the reveal of Lilith, and ever since Kaji brought Adam to Gendo in episode 8, I assumed that humanity had been born from Adam -- because Gendo called the sample the "first human" (a line that makes little sense ever since the Lilith-reveal). However, back then, I did not make the connection that humans could be the 2nd Angel, because it was not until EoE that the thought that humans could be considered an "Angel" ever entered my head (via Misato's description of them as such, even though it makes no sense because humans and Angels come from completely different progenitor beings and Lilith is not technically an Angel by the commonly-used definition because she wasn't born from Adam and OH GOD WHY CAN'T THESE WORDS JUST HAVE SEPARATE CONSISTENT MEANINGS WHEN THEY ALREADY HAD THEM). I remember what a loop the reveal of Lilith threw me for, because by that time I thought I'd had it all figured out: that humans and Angels came from the same progenitor being, Adam, and must fight each other because if any Angel were to reach Adam it would wipe out humanity. More than that, I liked it. I thought it tied well into the whole "humans and Angels are actually very similar and it's sad that they're killing each other" theme. In fact the first thought to run through my head when "Adam" was revealed as Lilith was: "...What is the point of that ?!" My neat little backstory I'd figured out for the story was now all messed up and, to my eyes, had just become needlessly complicated. I still prefer the backstory I'd thought up back then (and can easily extrapolate forward into EoE if the same theme had been continued). Just think of what it would mean for humanity to literally be just another Angel, born from Adam exactly as all the rest. Compared to the Lilim/Angel dichotomy it would have made Angel-killing analogous to fratricide, because humanity would be exactly as related to each Angel as each Angel was to each other , whereas in the series as it was, that extra fork in the family tree creates that extra layer of separation. It would have made Misato's statement in EoE that humanity is an Angel that much more poignant and shocking because it would have been that much more literally true. (Or maybe I don't prefer it. Would that all have been too predictable? Maybe not shocking at all? Too simple, too straightforward, compared to the ages of Discussion that resulted from the seemingly needless extra complications introduced by the introduction of Lilith? After all, what can compare to that shocking sense of "Everything I thought I knew and made sense of is wrong" that happened to me when Lilith was revealed? That might be the more thematically appropriate shock after all.) ...But that's all neither here nor there. To bring it back to Agememnon's point, yeah, I agree, it's entirely possible that the 2nd Angel was originally intended to be humanity, and the more I think about it the more likely it seems. Maybe the "humanity is the 18th Angel" line in EoE is some relic of that, taking the place of a line that had been planned originally to be "humanity is the 2nd Angel (you know, that 2nd Angel we never talked about the whole series, leading up to this big shocking reveal)".
